content:
Is there a way to insmod a rust kernel module into default (not precompiled with rust) image.
For example, when I try to install the module into Ubuntu 24.04 with kernel version 6.8.0.51-generic and the modinfo "vermagic: 6.8.0-rc5-dirty SMP preempt mod_unload" I get the next error: .gnu.linkonce.this_module section size must match the kernel's built struct module size at run time.

As I've found in Mentorship Session videos, there were no possibility to do this back in 2022.
Is this still true?

Is this still true?

Yes, out-of-tree modules require the base kernel to have CONFIG_RUST=y at the moment.
Mike has marked this topic as resolved.
